text.source_text: प्रकृतित्वस्याय मभ्युच्चयः । इतश्च प्रकृतिर्ब्रह्म , यत्कारणं साक्षाद्ब्रह्मैव कारणमुपादाय उभौ प्रभवप्रलयावाम्नायेते — ‘ सर्वाणि ह वा इमानि भूतान्याकाशादेव समुत्पद्यन्त आकाशं प्रत्यस्तं यन्ति ’ (छा. उ. १ । ९ । १) इति । यद्धि यस्मात्प्रभवति , यस्मिंश्च प्रलीयते तत्तस्योपादानं प्रसिद्धम् , यथा व्रीहियवादीनां पृथिवी । ‘ साक्षात् ’ इति च — उपादानान्तरानुपादानं दर्शयति ‘ आकाशादेव ’ इति । प्रत्यस्तमयश्च नोपादानादन्यत्र कार्यस्य दृष्टः ॥ २५ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: prakṛtitvasyāya mabhyuccayaḥ | itaśca prakṛtirbrahma , yatkāraṇaṃ sākṣādbrahmaiva kāraṇamupādāya ubhau prabhavapralayāvāmnāyete — ‘ sarvāṇi ha vā imāni bhūtānyākāśādeva samutpadyanta ākāśaṃ pratyastaṃ yanti ’ (chā. u. 1 | 9 | 1) iti | yaddhi yasmātprabhavati , yasmiṃśca pralīyate tattasyopādānaṃ prasiddham , yathā vrīhiyavādīnāṃ pṛthivī | ‘ sākṣāt ’ iti ca — upādānāntarānupādānaṃ darśayati ‘ ākāśādeva ’ iti | pratyastamayaśca nopādānādanyatra kāryasya dṛṣṭaḥ || 25 ||

translation.literal_translation: This is an additional proof of Brahman's material causality: scripture directly states both emergence and dissolution with Brahman itself as locus, as in passages saying all beings arise from and return to that very ground.
translation.technical_translation: Abhyuccaya argument: direct two-way scriptural predication (origin and reabsorption) centered in Brahman implies upadana identity rather than external substrate dependence.
translation.interpretive_note: Bidirectional emanation-reabsorption testimony strengthens material-cause reading.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Draft translation prioritized doctrinal continuity within the local adhikarana sequence.
Final philological pass should tighten citation-level granularity and preferred Sanskrit term retention.
